feat(openapi-display): add auth indicators and legend (bd-2buz)

Enhances the OpenAPI endpoint table display with:

- Auth requirement indicator (🔒) for endpoints with security requirements
- Deprecation indicator (⚠) for deprecated endpoints
- Legend section explaining indicators (only shown when relevant)
- Tag-based grouping helper method (for future use)

Indicators work in all output modes:
- Plain mode: [auth], [deprecated] text indicators
- Minimal mode: 🔒, ⚠ emoji indicators
- Rich mode: 🔒, ⚠ with styled legend

Added 6 new unit tests for indicator functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-27ju","title":"Add rich_rust dependency and feature flag integration","description":"# Add rich_rust Dependency Integration

## Task Overview

Configure the rich_rust dependency properly in fastapi-output, including feature flag
handling so that rich output can be optionally compiled out for minimal builds.

## Why Feature Flags Matter

1. **Binary Size**: rich_rust adds ~2MB+ to binary; agents may not need it
2. **Compile Time**: Faster builds when rich features disabled
3. **Dependency Control**: Some environments may not want rich_rust's transitive deps
4. **CI Optimization**: Can test plain mode without rich compilation

## Cargo.toml Configuration

```toml
[package]
name = "fastapi-output"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"

[dependencies]
# Terminal detection (always needed, small)
crossterm = "0.28"

# For global instance
once_cell = "1.19"

# Rich output library - optional
rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", optional = true }

[features]
default = ["rich"]
rich = ["dep:rich_rust"]
# Full features includes syntax highlighting etc
full = ["rich", "rich_rust/full"]
```

## Conditional Compilation Pattern

Throughout the crate, use this pattern:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "rich")]
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

impl RichOutput {
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    fn render_table_rich(&self, headers: &[&str], rows: &[Vec<String>]) {
        // Use rich_rust Table
    }
    
    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
    fn render_table_rich(&self, headers: &[&str], rows: &[Vec<String>]) {
        // Fallback to plain
        self.render_table_plain(headers, rows);
    }
}
```

## Feature Detection at Runtime

```rust
/// Check if rich output is available at compile time.
pub const fn has_rich_support() -> bool {
    cfg!(feature = "rich")
}

/// Get description of available features.
pub fn feature_info() -> &'static str {
    if cfg!(feature = "full") {
        "full (rich output with syntax highlighting)"
    } else if cfg!(feature = "rich") {
        "rich (styled output with tables and panels)"
    } else {
        "plain (text only, no dependencies)"
    }
}
```

## Usage in fastapi Crate

The main fastapi crate should re-export with same feature flags:

```toml
# crates/fastapi/Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
fastapi-output = { path = "../fastapi-output", optional = true }

[features]
default = ["output"]
output = ["dep:fastapi-output", "fastapi-output/rich"]
output-plain = ["dep:fastapi-output"]
full = ["output", "fastapi-output/full"]
```

## Build Verification

```bash
# Build with rich (default)
cargo build -p fastapi-output

# Build without rich (plain only)
cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features

# Build with full features
cargo build -p fastapi-output --features full

# Verify size difference
ls -la target/release/libfastapi_output.rlib  # With rich
ls -la target/release/libfastapi_output.rlib  # Without rich
```

## Graceful Degradation

When rich feature is disabled:
- OutputMode::Rich becomes OutputMode::Plain automatically
- All rich methods have plain fallbacks
- No rich_rust types in public API
- Compile-time enforcement (no runtime panics)

## Test Matrix

| Feature Flags | Rich Available | Default Mode |
|---------------|----------------|--------------|
| default (rich) | Yes | Auto-detect |
| none | No | Plain |
| full | Yes + syntax | Auto-detect |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. cargo build succeeds with default features
2. cargo build succeeds with --no-default-features
3. cargo build succeeds with --features full
4. Rich mode unavailable when feature disabled
5. No compile errors in either configuration
6. Feature detection functions work correctly
7.  {"id":"bd-29lr","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - fastapi-output Crate and Core Infrastructure","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Purpose

Establish the core infrastructure for rich output integration by creating the new fastapi-output 
crate with agent detection, mode switching, and the RichOutput facade API.

## Why This Phase First

Everything else depends on this foundation:
- Agent detection determines which mode to use
- OutputMode enum is used by all components
- RichOutput facade is the unified API all other phases call
- Theme system defines colors used everywhere

Without this phase, no other rich output work can proceed.

## Deliverables

1. **New crate: fastapi-output** (crates/fastapi-output/)
   - Cargo.toml with rich_rust as optional dependency
   - Module structure for all subcomponents
   - Re-exports for convenient API access

2. **Agent Detection System** (detection.rs)
   - is_agent_environment() function
   - Environment variable checks (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, etc.)
   - TTY detection via crossterm
   - NO_COLOR standard support
   - FORCE_COLOR override

3. **OutputMode System** (mode.rs)
   - OutputMode enum (Rich, Plain, Minimal)
   - Mode selection logic
   - Runtime mode switching support

4. **RichOutput Facade** (facade.rs)
   - Central RichOutput struct
   - Mode-aware method dispatching
   - Global instance management (lazy static)
   - Builder pattern for configuration

5. **Theme System** (themes.rs)
   - FastApiTheme struct with all color definitions
   - ThemePreset enum for built-in themes
   - Color constants for HTTP methods, status codes
   - Theme loading and application

## Technical Decisions

### Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Modularity**: Can be disabled entirely via feature flag
2. **Compile Time**: Only compiled when needed
3. **Dependency Isolation**: rich_rust dependency contained
4. **Testing**: Can test output logic independently

### Why Optional rich_rust Dependency?

When the "rich" feature is disabled:
- No rich_rust code compiled
- Plain mode is the only mode
- Minimal binary size impact
- Zero runtime overhead

### Global Instance vs Passed Reference?

Using a global lazy_static instance because:
- Output happens everywhere in the codebase
- Passing RichOutput through every function is impractical
- One-time initialization is fine for this use case
- Thread-safe via LazyLock

## Files to Create

```
crates/fastapi-output/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs           # Public API, prelude
│   ├── detection.rs     # Agent environment detection
│   ├── mode.rs          # OutputMode enum and logic
│   ├── facade.rs        # RichOutput struct
│   └── themes.rs        # Theme definitions
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
2. `cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features` succeeds (plain mode only)
3. Agent detection correctly identifies Claude Code, Codex, etc.
4. Mode switching works at runtime
5. Theme colors render correctly in rich mode
6. -{"id":"bd-2buz","title":"Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display","description":"## Task: OpenAPI Endpoint Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create a visual table display of all OpenAPI-documented endpoints, showing methods, paths, summaries, and tags in a scannable format.

### Background & Reasoning
When developing APIs, having a quick overview of all endpoints helps:
- Verify all routes are documented
- Check for naming consistency
- Understand API scope at a glance
- Debug missing endpoints

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/openapi_display.rs

pub struct OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo>,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub show_responses: bool,
}

pub struct EndpointInfo {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub operation_id: Option<String>,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub deprecated: bool,
    pub auth_required: bool,
    pub response_codes: Vec<u16>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌──────────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────────┐
│                                                                │
│ 12 endpoints documented                                        │
│                                                                │
│ Users                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /users              │ List all users               ││
│ │ POST   │ /users              │ Create a new user     🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /users/{id}         │ Get user by ID               ││
│ │ PUT    │ /users/{id}         │ Update user           🔒     ││
│ │ DELETE │ /users/{id}         │ Delete user           🔒     ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ Items                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /items              │ List all items               ││
│ │ POST   │ /items              │ Create an item        🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /items/{id}         │ Get item by ID               ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                                │
│ 🔒 = Authentication required                                   │
│ ⚠ = Deprecated                                                │
│                                                                │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
API Endpoints (12 total):

[Users]
  GET    /users             List all users
  POST   /users             Create a new user [auth]
  GET    /users/{id}        Get user by ID
  PUT    /users/{id}        Update user [auth]
  DELETE /users/{id}        Delete user [auth]

[Items]
  GET    /items             List all items
  POST   /items             Create an item [auth]
  GET    /items/{id}        Get item by ID
```

### Implementation
```rust
impl OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!("  {} endpoints documented", self.endpoints.len()));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Group by tag if enabled
        let groups = if self.group_by_tag {
            self.group_endpoints_by_tag()
        } else {
            vec![("All".to_string(), self.endpoints.clone())]
        };
        
        for (tag, endpoints) in groups {
            if self.group_by_tag {
                content.push(theme.style_text(&format!("  {}", tag), "bold"));
            }
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(8))
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(25))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(40));
            
            for ep in &endpoints {
                let method_styled = theme.style_method(&ep.method);
                
                let mut summary = ep.summary.clone().unwrap_or_default();
                
                // Add indicators
                let mut indicators = Vec::new();
                if ep.auth_required {
                    indicators.push("🔒");
                }
                if ep.deprecated {
                    indicators.push("⚠");
                }
                
                if !indicators.is_empty() {
                    summary = format!("{} {}", summary, indicators.join(" "));
                }
                
                table.add_row_cells([
                    method_styled,
                    ep.path.clone(),
                    summary,
                ]);
            }
            
            content.push(format!("{}", table.render_to_string()));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Legend
        content.push(theme.style_text("  🔒 = Authentication required", "dim"));
        content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ = Deprecated", "dim"));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("API Endpoints")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with OpenAPI Crate
```rust
// In fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs

impl OpenApiSpec {
    pub fn display_endpoints(&self) {
        let endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo> = self.paths.iter()
            .flat_map(|(path, ops)| {
                ops.iter().map(|(method, op)| EndpointInfo {
                    method: method.clone(),
                    path: path.clone(),
                    operation_id: op.operation_id.clone(),
                    summary: op.summary.clone(),
                    tags: op.tags.clone(),
                    deprecated: op.deprecated,
                    auth_required: !op.security.is_empty(),
                    response_codes: op.responses.keys().cloned().collect(),
                })
            })
            .collect();
        
        OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
            endpoints,
            group_by_tag: true,
            show_responses: false,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EndpointInfo data structure
- [ ] Table display with method coloring
- [ ] Tag-based grouping (optional)
- [ ] Auth requirement indicator (🔒)
- [ ] Deprecation indicator (⚠)
- [ ] Endpoint count summary
- [ ] Legend for indicators
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with OpenAPI spec
- [ ] Unit tests

### Dependencies

 {"id":"bd-3ihr","title":"Router: Wildcard catch-all route support ({*path})","description":"Router doesn't support catch-all wildcard routes like '/files/{*path}' that match any remaining path segments. Essential for file serving and SPA routing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:51:29.208480863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795218911Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795153479Z","close_reason":"Implemented {*path} wildcard syntax for catch-all routes. Added 13 comprehensive tests covering file serving, SPA routing, priority, conflicts, and syntax equivalence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:04.030031370Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
Wildcard/catch-all routes like `/files/{*path}` match any remaining path segments. Essential for:
- Static file serving: `/static/{*filepath}` matches `/static/js/app.js`
- SPA routing: `/{*route}` catches all frontend routes for client-side router
- Proxy routes: `/api/{*rest}` forwards to backend

## Current Limitation
Router only supports single-segment parameters `{id}`. No way to match multiple segments.

## Solution Architecture

### Route Pattern Syntax
```
/files/{*path}     -> matches /files/a, /files/a/b, /files/a/b/c
/api/v1/{*rest}    -> captures everything after /api/v1/
```

### Trie Implementation
Wildcard nodes are special leaf nodes that match any remaining path:
```rust
enum TrieNode {
    Static { segment: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Param { name: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Wildcard { name: String, route: Route },  // Always a leaf
}
```

### Matching Priority
1. Static segments (exact match)
2. Single-segment parameters
3. Wildcard catch-all (lowest priority)

### Captured Value
Wildcard captures entire remaining path:
```rust
// Route: /files/{*path}
// Request: /files/css/styles/main.css
// Captured: path = "css/styles/main.css"
```

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Trie and Router
- crates/fastapi-router/src/trie.rs (if separate)

## Dependencies
- Should coordinate with bd-5bt0 (route priority rules)
- Should update bd-2b8n (type converters) - wildcard is always String

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `{*name}` syntax recognized in route patterns
- [ ] Wildcard matches zero or more segments
- [ ] Captured value is complete remaining path
- [ ] Wildcard has lowest priority (static > param > wildcard)
- [ ] Only one wildcard per route (at end)
- [ ] Tests cover edge cases

 {"id":"bd-3jgt","title":"CRITICAL: Fix rich_rust dependency path in fastapi-output","description":"The rich_rust dependency in fastapi-output/Cargo.toml uses a broken path '../../../rich_rust' which doesn't exist. This blocks the entire rich output feature from compiling.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:48:58.676457053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:45.315586191Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:38.361563448Z","close_reason":"Fixed rich_rust dependency - using git dependency, re-enabled fastapi-output in workspace, fixed compile errors in routing_debug.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","depends_on_id":"bd-nww8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:40.385854229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The fastapi-output crate provides rich terminal output for development (colored logs, ASCII banners, styled errors). It depends on rich_rust for the terminal rendering capabilities.

## Current Problem
In crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
path = "../../../rich_rust"  # This path doesn't exist!
optional = true
```

The path assumes rich_rust is a sibling of the fastapi_rust workspace root, but:
1. rich_rust may not be checked out at that location
2. This breaks `cargo build` for the entire workspace when the feature is enabled
3. CI/CD pipelines will fail

## Impact
- `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` fails
- Cannot test or use rich output features
- Blocks Phase 2+ of rich output integration
- Makes fastapi-output effectively unusable

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Use crates.io (Recommended if published)
```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { version = "0.1", optional = true }
```
Pros: Standard, portable, versioned
Cons: Requires rich_rust to be published

### Option 2: Git dependency
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
git = "https://github.com/user/rich_rust"
optional = true
```
Pros: Works before crates.io publish
Cons: Slower builds, no offline

### Option 3: Workspace member
Move rich_rust into this workspace as a crate
Pros: Full control, workspace optimization
Cons: Bloats this repo if rich_rust is separate project

### Option 4: Conditional path with fallback
```toml
[target.'cfg(feature = "rich-local")'.dependencies]
rich_rust = { path = "../rich_rust" }
```
For development only

## Recommendation
1. Short-term: Option 2 (git dependency) to unblock development
2. Long-term: Option 1 (crates.io) when rich_rust is published

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
- [ ] `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` succeeds (if feature exists)
- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Document the dependency strategy in README

## Dependencies
- Requires rich_rust crate to be available (published or git)

## Considerations
- rich_rust is Dan's custom crate - need to verify it's ready for external use
- May need to fork/vendor if not available
- Consider making rich optional with fallback to plain output

Added Handler import, Arc<dyn Handler> field, custom Debug impl, Route::new() with handler param, with_arc_handler(), handler() getter, PlaceholderHandler for macros. All 59 router tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-229o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:59:31.910506135Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:03.014972651Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The Route struct in fastapi-router/src/lib.rs currently stores route metadata (method, pattern, converter, path) but is missing the most essential field - the handler function pointer that should be invoked when a route matches.

## Current Implementation Gap
```rust
pub struct Route {
    pub method: Method,
    pub pattern: String,
    pub converter: Option<&'static str>,
    pub path: String,
    // MISSING: handler field!
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Routes can be registered and matched, but there's no way to dispatch to handlers
- The Router.lookup() returns Option<&Route> but Route contains no handler
- This makes the entire framework non-functional for its primary purpose
- ALL other features depend on this working (extractors, middleware, responses)

## Solution Architecture
The handler field should store a type-erased async function. Options considered:

### Option 1: Box<dyn Fn> (Recommended)
```rust
type Handler = Box<dyn Fn(&Cx, Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>> + Send + Sync>;
```
Pros: Simple, works with closures
Cons: Double indirection, heap allocation per call

### Option 2: Arc<dyn HandlerTrait>
```rust
trait Handler: Send + Sync {
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>>;
}
```
Pros: Allows shared handlers, extensible
Cons: More complexity

### Option 3: Generic Handler<H>
Pros: Zero-cost, monomorphized
Cons: Requires type erasure at registration anyway

## Recommendation
Use Option 1 with a Handler type alias. Simple, works, can optimize later.

## Files to Modify
1. crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Add handler field to Route, update Router
2. May need Handler trait in fastapi-core for reuse

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route struct has handler field
- [ ] Router::add() accepts handler parameter
- [ ] Router::lookup() returns route with callable handler
- [ ] Handler works with async functions via Cx
- [ ] All existing router tests pass
- [ ] New tests verify handler dispatch

## Dependencies
None - this is foundational

## Estimated Complexity
Medium - straightforward addition but needs careful async handling

impl FastApiTheme {
    pub fn style_method(&self, method: &HttpMethod) -> String {
        let color = match method {
            HttpMethod::Get => &self.colors.method_get,
            HttpMethod::Post => &self.colors.method_post,
            HttpMethod::Put => &self.colors.method_put,
            HttpMethod::Patch => &self.colors.method_patch,
            HttpMethod::Delete => &self.colors.method_delete,
            HttpMethod::Options => &self.colors.method_options,
            HttpMethod::Head => &self.colors.method_head,
        };
        
        let style = Style::new().foreground(*color).bold();
        format!("{:7}", method.as_str()).with_style(style)
    }
    
    pub fn style_status(&self, status: u16) -> String {
        let color = match status {
            100..=199 => &self.colors.status_1xx,
            200..=299 => &self.colors.status_2xx,
            300..=399 => &self.colors.status_3xx,
            400..=499 => &self.colors.status_4xx,
            500..=599 => &self.colors.status_5xx,
            _ => &self.colors.text_primary,
        };
        
        let style = Style::new().foreground(*color);
        format!("{} {}", status, status_reason(status)).with_style(style)
    }
    
    pub fn create_panel(&self, content: &str) -> Panel {
        Panel::from_text(content)
            .box_style(self.box_style.to_rich_rust_style())
            .border_style(Style::new().foreground(self.colors.primary))
    }

    }
}

//\! Error formatting components.

use crate::{RichOutput, OutputMode};

/// A single validation error.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ValidationError {
    pub loc: Vec<String>,
    pub msg: String,
    pub input: Option<String>,
}

impl ValidationError {
    pub fn new(loc: Vec<String>, msg: &str) -> Self {
        Self { loc, msg: msg.to_string(), input: None }
    }
    
    pub fn with_input(mut self, input: &str) -> Self {
        self.input = Some(input.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn loc_string(&self) -> String {
        self.loc.join(".")
    }
}

/// Validation error collection for display.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,
    pub context: Option<String>,
}

impl ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub fn new(errors: Vec<ValidationError>) -> Self {
        Self { errors, context: None }
    }
    
    pub fn with_context(mut self, ctx: &str) -> Self {
        self.context = Some(ctx.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn render(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] ValidationErrorDisplay::render() errors={}", self.errors.len());
        match output.mode() {
            OutputMode::Rich => self.render_rich(output),
            OutputMode::Plain | OutputMode::Minimal => self.render_plain(output),
        }
    }
    
    fn render_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        let ctx = self.context.as_deref().unwrap_or("request");
        output.print(&format\!("Validation Error: {} errors for {}", self.errors.len(), ctx));
        for err in &self.errors {
            let loc = err.loc_string();
            let input_str = err.input.as_ref()
                .map(|i| format\!(" (input: \"{}\")", i))
                .unwrap_or_default();
            output.print(&format\!("  {}: {}{}", loc, err.msg, input_str));
        }
    }
    
    fn render_rich(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        // For now, use plain. Rich would use panels.
        self.render_plain(output);
    }
    
    pub fn as_plain_text(&self) -> String {
        let ctx = self.context.as_deref().unwrap_or("request");
        let mut lines = vec\![];
        lines.push(format\!("Validation Error: {} errors for {}", self.errors.len(), ctx));
        for err in &self.errors {
            let loc = err.loc_string();
            let input_str = err.input.as_ref()
                .map(|i| format\!(" (input: \"{}\")", i))
                .unwrap_or_default();
            lines.push(format\!("  {}: {}{}", loc, err.msg, input_str));
        }
        lines.join("\n")
    }
}

/// HTTP error for display.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct HttpErrorDisplay {
    pub status_code: u16,
    pub status_text: String,
    pub detail: String,
    pub path: Option<String>,
    pub method: Option<String>,
    pub request_id: Option<String>,
}

impl HttpErrorDisplay {
    pub fn new(status_code: u16, detail: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            status_code,
            status_text: status_text(status_code),
            detail: detail.to_string(),
            path: None,
            method: None,
            request_id: None,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn with_path(mut self, path: &str) -> Self {
        self.path = Some(path.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn with_method(mut self, method: &str) -> Self {
        self.method = Some(method.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn with_request_id(mut self, id: &str) -> Self {
        self.request_id = Some(id.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn render(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] HttpErrorDisplay::render() status={}", self.status_code);
        match output.mode() {
            OutputMode::Rich => self.render_rich(output),
            OutputMode::Plain | OutputMode::Minimal => self.render_plain(output),
        }
    }
    
    fn render_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        output.print(&format\!("HTTP Error: {} {}", self.status_code, self.status_text));
        output.print(&format\!("  Detail: {}", self.detail));
        if let Some(path) = &self.path {
            output.print(&format\!("  Path: {}", path));
        }
        if let Some(method) = &self.method {
            output.print(&format\!("  Method: {}", method));
        }
        if let Some(id) = &self.request_id {
            output.print(&format\!("  Request-ID: {}", id));
        }
    }
    
    fn render_rich(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        self.render_plain(output);
    }
    
    pub fn as_plain_text(&self) -> String {
        let mut lines = vec\![];
        lines.push(format\!("HTTP Error: {} {}", self.status_code, self.status_text));
        lines.push(format\!("  Detail: {}", self.detail));
        if let Some(path) = &self.path {
            lines.push(format\!("  Path: {}", path));
        }
        if let Some(method) = &self.method {
            lines.push(format\!("  Method: {}", method));
        }
        lines.join("\n")
    }
}

fn status_text(code: u16) -> String {
    match code {
        400 => "Bad Request".to_string(),
        401 => "Unauthorized".to_string(),
        403 => "Forbidden".to_string(),
        404 => "Not Found".to_string(),
        405 => "Method Not Allowed".to_string(),
        422 => "Unprocessable Entity".to_string(),
        500 => "Internal Server Error".to_string(),
        502 => "Bad Gateway".to_string(),
        503 => "Service Unavailable".to_string(),
        _ => format\!("Error {}", code),
    }
}

        assert_eq\!(err.loc, vec\!["body", "email"]);

        assert\!(err.input.is_none());

            .with_input("null");

        assert_eq\!(err.input, Some("null".to_string()));
    }

        assert_eq\!(loc, "body.user.email");
